tagline: Now generally available for enhanced workspace security.
what_it_does: Context-based ingress control allows account admins to define allow and deny rules that combine user identities, network sources, and request types to manage access to Databricks workspaces. This feature works alongside existing IP access lists and enables granular control over who can access workspace endpoints. It supports both public and private access policies and can be configured at the account level to govern multiple workspaces simultaneously.
why_it_matters: This update enhances security by ensuring that only trusted combinations of identity and network source can access sensitive workspace resources, thereby reducing the risk of unauthorized access. It benefits organizations by providing a flexible and detailed access control mechanism that can adapt to various user scenarios, improving governance and compliance while minimizing potential security breaches.
plain_summary: You can now control who accesses your Databricks workspaces based on specific rules for users and networks.

capabilities.title: Granular Access Control
capabilities.desc: Define specific rules based on user identity, network source, and request type.

capabilities.title: Audit Logging
capabilities.desc: Capture detailed logs of denied requests for monitoring and compliance.

capabilities.title: Flexible Policy Enforcement
capabilities.desc: Choose between enforced rules and dry run mode to evaluate policy impacts.

capabilities.title: Support for Multiple Workspaces
capabilities.desc: Manage ingress policies at the account level to apply to multiple workspaces.

prerequisites: Requires the Enterprise tier of Databricks.
use_cases: Restrict access from untrusted networks by requiring trusted network sources.
Allow SaaS clients with dynamic IPs to access Databricks resources securely.
Limit access to specific APIs or UI components based on user identity.
Protect sensitive automation by restricting service principals to trusted networks.
limitations: Account-level context-based ingress policy is currently in Beta.
Denials for account-policy are not yet logged.

steps.title: Access Ingress Policies
steps.desc: Navigate to the context-based ingress policies management section.

steps.title: Define Rules
steps.desc: Create allow and deny rules based on identities and network sources.

steps.title: Set Enforcement Mode
steps.desc: Choose between enforced rules or dry run mode for testing.

steps.title: Monitor Logs
steps.desc: Review logs in the system.access.inbound_network table for denied requests.

steps.title: Apply to Workspaces
steps.desc: Assign the ingress policy to the desired workspaces.

key_takeaway: Context-based ingress control provides robust security for Databricks workspaces by allowing precise access management based on user identity and network context.
